title: Don't Miss These!
intro: For the most part these songs came out in June.

songs.title: 2 Many Days
songs.review: If you like SZA, this song is for you. If you're into R&B, this song is for you. As a huge Steve Lacy fan, it only made sense for me to start exploring more of Syd's music. I'm familiar with some of The Internet's biggest records, but I haven't done a deep dive into their discography yet. I've really enjoyed the singles Syd has released for this project, and I absolutely love this one. I'm definitely going to check out her new album when it drops on the 16th. I also have to give her a shoutout for producing this song, along with every other track on the album.

songs.title: Valentine’s Day Freestyle ’26
songs.review:
Why is Peggy dropping a Valentine's Freestyle in June as part of his deluxe? I don't know. But one thing I do know is that if it's a Valentine's Day Freestyle, it's going to be good. Last year, he gave us an amazing song on the actual day. Hopefully, he keeps the tradition going because I can't get enough of these.

Every time I hear Peggy rapping over an acoustic guitar and sample, I automatically add the song to my playlist. The first beat drop is a little jarring, especially if you don't normally listen to him, but before you know it, the song is over. I promise this won't scare the hoes, but it might heighten their senses a little bit. His flow during the second verse is so smooth as he effortlessly blends each line into the next. Peggy tends to  let the instrumental breathe in the middle of songs and this a great example of that. I love this song, and it is much better than most of the songs on this album.

lines.text: Liquidating your assets, the money and the magic
Lavish under the rock, that pink like Patrick
Ice everywhere with the stick, I'm hitting a hat trick
You niggas don't even mind the cars that you be crashin'

songs.title: MOVE YOUR BODY
songs.review: He probably moonwalked into the booth because he had to be feeling like Mike when he made this. This song is an upbeat, '80s-style pop anthem that makes you want to dance.
